# UC-10.11.44 · CrowdStrike Real-Time Response Audit Trail

## Description

RTR auditing is critical because live response can access sensitive hosts.

## Value

RTR auditing is critical because live response can access sensitive hosts.

## Implementation

Log every RTR command with analyst identity. Review privileged actions for insider and compromise scenarios.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-crowdstrike-falcon`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=crowdstrike:rr`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Log every RTR command with analyst identity. Review privileged actions for insider and compromise scenarios.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=audit sourcetype="crowdstrike:rr"
| stats count by command, user, target_host
| search command IN ("put","get","runscript","map")
```

Understanding this SPL

**CrowdStrike Real-Time Response Audit Trail** — RTR auditing is critical because live response can access sensitive hosts.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=crowdstrike:rr`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-crowdstrike-falcon`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: audit; **sourcetype**: crowdstrike:rr. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=audit, sourcetype="crowdstrike:rr". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by command, user, target_host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Splunk_Audit by _time span=1h | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**CrowdStrike Real-Time Response Audit Trail** — RTR auditing is critical because live response can access sensitive hosts.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=crowdstrike:rr`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-crowdstrike-falcon`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Splunk_Audit` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (commands), Timeline.

## SPL

```spl
index=audit sourcetype="crowdstrike:rr"
| stats count by command, user, target_host
| search command IN ("put","get","runscript","map")
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Splunk_Audit by _time span=1h | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (commands), Timeline.
